LOCAL AUTHORITY PROCUREMENT NOTICE

Solicitation Title: Integrated Council Data Analytics Platform
Procurement Reference: LA-2026-DATA-001
Contracting Authority: Metropolitan London Borough Council
Published Date: 15 March 2026
Closing Date: 15 May 2026 12:00 noon
Estimated Contract Value: £2.8 Million
Contract Duration: 24 months

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The Metropolitan London Borough Council seeks an integrated data analytics platform to consolidate resident data across five key service areas: Council Tax & Benefits, Adult Social Care, Children's Services, Housing, and Planning & Enforcement. Current systems operate in silos, preventing holistic analysis and targeted service delivery.

PROJECT SCOPE

Data Integration:
- Consolidate data from 8 legacy council systems (Oracle EBS, various case management systems)
- Match records at household and individual level (pseudonymised)
- Support both structured (transactional) and unstructured (case notes) data
- Design for incremental expansion to additional service areas

Analytics & Insights:
- Build interactive dashboards for service managers and elected members
- Implement predictive models for vulnerability identification (safeguarding priority)
- Generate monthly performance reports against council targets
- Enable ad-hoc analysis capability for commissioners and analysts

Data Governance:
- Implement role-based access control across service silos
- Ensure GDPR compliance with Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)
- Maintain audit trail for all data access and modifications
- Support data retention and secure archival policies

Technical Requirements:
- Cloud-based SaaS deployment (Azure, AWS, or GCP acceptable)
- Minimum 99.5% uptime for production environment
- Sub-5 second query response times for standard dashboards
- Support 50+ concurrent users across council departments
- Daily data refresh cycles from source systems

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS

1. Handle datasets from 8 different source systems
2. Support 50+ concurrent users with role-based access control
3. Dashboard query response time <5 seconds
4. GDPR compliance with documented DPIA
5. Data integration completion within 4 months of go-live
6. Support for both SQL and no-code dashboard creation
7. Automated daily reconciliation and data quality checks
8. Disaster recovery with 4-hour RTO and 1-hour RPO

OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS

- Machine learning for service demand forecasting
- Integration with citizen portals for self-service access
- Mobile app for field workers in social care
- API for third-party integration with care providers
- Advanced geospatial analysis for neighbourhood targeting

EVALUATION CRITERIA

Technical Approach and Capability: 45%
Previous Experience with Local Government: 25%
Team Qualifications and Support Model: 20%
Price: 10%

Submit to: procurements@metroborough.gov.uk
